name: run_control
documentation:
Run-level control helpers for plugins.

## API Reference

### await_user
Type: function
Source: __init__.py
Signature: await_user(message: str, *, patch: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> str

Ask the kernel for user input and return the answer to this plugin call.

### stop
Type: function
Source: __init__.py
Signature: stop(reason: str = '', *, response: str | None = None, patch: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> NoReturn

Stop the current run successfully and skip remaining execution.

### abort
Type: function
Source: __init__.py
Signature: abort(reason: str, *, patch: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> NoReturn

Abort the current run with a reason.

### set_input
Type: function
Source: __init__.py
Signature: set_input(value: str) -> None

Request that the kernel replace Run Context input with a new value.

### patch_context
Type: function
Source: __init__.py
Signature: patch_context(patch: dict[str, Any]) -> None

Accumulate a patch that the kernel will merge into Run Context.

### consume_patch
Type: function
Source: __init__.py
Signature: consume_patch() -> dict[str, Any]

Return and clear the accumulated Run Context patch.
